title_en: 91. Be brave and start from now! (cont.)
content_en: I recall being invited to a wedding ceremony once where a bright young man approached me and greeted me very warmly, and then reminded me of his childhood memories of me when I came to his school to deliver a lecture. Sometimes we even notice children who have been treated kindly by someone bringing their parents to him or her and introducing them in the hope that the parents have the same love and respect for the man or woman that they do. I would not hide the fact that I am very kind and welcoming of children. I make it a point to attentively listen to their sweet conversations, even though they are usually of no substance. In fact, sometimes I am extremely welcoming to them simply in order to win the hearts of their parents. I used to meet a friend of mine every now and then who would have his son with him, and I would be extremely kind and With children playful with the son. One day, this friend of mine met me at a wedding party with this son. He greeted me and said, “What have you done to my son? His teacher at school asked his pupils what they would like to be when they grow up. Some said doctors, others said engineers, but my son said: ‘I want to be Muhammad al-‘Ariﬁ: You can sometimes notice the different ways in which people deal with children. When a person enters a public gathering with his son, he shakes everyone’s hand one by one, while his son follows suit. Amongst the people are those who would completely ignore the child, others would barely shake his hand, and others would warmly shake his hand saying, “Welcome, young man! How are you today?” It is the love of that person that will be engraved in the heart of the child, as well as in the hearts of his parents. The Prophet  would display the best form of treatment with children. Anas bin Malik – may Allah be pleased with him – had a younger brother, and the Prophet  would play with him and nickname him Abu ‘Umayr. The child had a small bird that he played with. Whenever the Prophet  would meet him, he would joke with him saying, “O Abu ‘Umayr, what happened to al-Nughayr?” meaning, the bird. He would be kind and playful with children. Whenever he would meet Zaynab bint Umm Salamah, he would playfully say to her, “O Zuwaynib! O Zuwaynib!” Whenever he passed by children playing, he would greet them. Whenever he visited the Ansaar, he would greet their children and place his hand on their heads out of compassion. Upon the return of the Muslim army With children from Mu’tah, the Prophet  along with the rest of the Muslims and their children met them on their way back to Madinah. When the Prophet  saw the children he said, “Take the children and carry them, and please pass me Ibn Ja’far.” They passed ‘Abdullah bin Ja’far on to him and he held him in his arms. One day as the Prophet  was performing ablution, there came to him Mahmud bin al-Rabi’ who was only ﬁve years old. The Prophet  took some water in his mouth and jokingly blew it out in his face. (al-Bukhari) Generally, the Prophet  was lively and merry with everyone. He would always try to make people happy. He would always be easygoing with everyone so that people would not become bored in his company. A man came to the Prophet  wanting a camel to ride on for a journey or expedition. The Prophet  said to him jokingly, “I will give you an offspring of a camel.” The man became surprised and thought of how he could possibly ride a camel’s offspring that could not carry his weight. He said, “O Messenger of Allah! What would I do with a camel’s offspring?” The Prophet  said, ‘Does a camel give birth to anything but an offspring?’ Meaning; I will give you a grown camel, however, no doubt, it is still another camel’s offspring! Once, he jokingly said to Anas, “O you, the possessor of two ears!” A woman once came to the Prophet  complaining about her husband, so he said to her, “Is your husband the one whose eyes are white?” The woman became worried and thought that her husband had become blind, in light of what Allah said about With children Jacob, “His eyes whitened from grieving so much...” meaning, he became blind. She went back to her husband terriﬁed and began to look into his eyes carefully. He asked her what the problem was. She said, “The Prophet  said that there is whiteness in your eyes!” The man said, “O woman! Did he not tell you that the whiteness in my eyes is more than the blackness?” meaning; everyone has whiteness and blackness in his eyes. The Prophet  would react positively to whoever joked or bantered with him and he would smile.
content_id: Aku ingat pernah diundang ke sebuah pesta pernikahan di mana seorang pemuda cerdas mendatangiku dan menyambutku dengan sangat hangat, lalu mengingatkan aku akan kenangan masa kecilnya ketika aku datang ke sekolahnya untuk menyampaikan ceramah. Kadang kita bahkan melihat anak-anak yang diperlakukan dengan baik oleh seseorang membawa orang tua mereka kepadanya dan memperkenalkan mereka dengan harapan orang tua memiliki rasa cinta dan penghormatan yang sama terhadap orang tersebut seperti yang mereka rasakan. Aku tidak menyembunyikan fakta bahwa aku sangat ramah dan terbuka kepada anak-anak. Aku selalu berusaha mendengarkan percakapan mereka yang manis dengan penuh perhatian, meskipun biasanya tidak berisi hal-hal yang substansial. Bahkan, kadang-kadang aku sangat menyambut mereka semata-mata untuk memenangkan hati orang tua mereka. Aku biasa bertemu seorang temanku dari waktu ke waktu yang selalu membawa anaknya, dan aku sangat ramah dan bercanda dengan sang anak. Suatu hari, temanku ini bertemu denganku di sebuah pesta pernikahan bersama anaknya. Ia menyapaku dan berkata, "Apa yang telah kamu lakukan kepada anakku? Guru di sekolahnya bertanya kepada murid-muridnya apa yang ingin mereka jadikan ketika besar nanti. Ada yang bilang dokter, ada yang bilang insinyur, tapi anakku bilang: 'Aku ingin menjadi Muhammad al-'Arifi!'" Kadang kamu bisa memperhatikan cara-cara berbeda dalam memperlakukan anak-anak. Ketika seseorang memasuki sebuah pertemuan umum bersama anaknya, ia menjabat tangan semua orang satu per satu, sementara anaknya mengikutinya. Di antara mereka ada yang sama sekali mengabaikan anak tersebut, ada yang hampir tidak menjabat tangannya, dan ada yang hangat menjabat tangannya sambil berkata, "Selamat datang, anak muda! Apa kabar hari ini?" Cinta orang itu akan terukir di hati anak tersebut, begitu pula di hati orang tuanya. Nabi menampilkan perlakuan terbaik terhadap anak-anak. Anas bin Malik — semoga Allah meridhainya — memiliki adik laki-laki, dan Nabi sering bermain dengannya dan memanggilnya dengan julukan Abu 'Umayr. Anak itu memiliki seekor burung kecil yang ia mainkan. Setiap kali Nabi bertemu dengannya, beliau bercanda dengannya seraya bertanya, "Wahai Abu 'Umayr, apa yang terjadi dengan al-Nughayr?" maksudnya, burung tersebut. Beliau bersikap ramah dan bermain bersama anak-anak. Setiap kali beliau bertemu Zaynab binti Umm Salamah, beliau bercanda berkata kepadanya, "Wahai Zuwaynib! Wahai Zuwaynib!" Setiap kali beliau melewati anak-anak yang sedang bermain, beliau mengucapkan salam kepada mereka. Setiap kali beliau mengunjungi kaum Anshar, beliau mengucapkan salam kepada anak-anak mereka dan meletakkan tangannya di kepala mereka sebagai tanda kasih sayang. Setelah pasukan Muslim kembali bersama para wanita dari Mu'tah, Nabi bersama kaum Muslimin dan anak-anak mereka menyambut mereka dalam perjalanan kembali ke Madinah. Ketika Nabi melihat anak-anak, beliau bersabda, "Ambil anak-anak itu dan gendong mereka, dan berikan kepadaku Ibnu Ja'far." Mereka menyerahkan 'Abdullah bin Ja'far kepadanya dan beliau menggendongnya. Suatu hari ketika Nabi sedang berwudhu, datanglah Mahmud bin al-Rabi' yang baru berusia lima tahun. Nabi mengambil air di mulutnya dan bercanda menyemburkannya ke wajah anak tersebut. (al-Bukhari) Umumnya, Nabi selalu bersemangat dan gembira bersama semua orang. Beliau selalu berusaha membuat orang bahagia. Beliau selalu mudah bergaul dengan semua orang agar orang tidak merasa bosan di dekatnya. Seorang lelaki datang kepada Nabi meminta seekor unta untuk ditunggangi dalam suatu perjalanan atau ekspedisi. Nabi berkata kepadanya dengan bercanda, "Aku akan memberikan kepadamu anak seekor unta." Lelaki itu menjadi heran dan berpikir bagaimana ia bisa menunggangi anak unta yang tidak bisa menanggung bebannya. Ia berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah! Apa yang akan aku lakukan dengan anak seekor unta?" Nabi bersabda, 'Apakah seekor unta melahirkan selain anaknya?' Maksudnya; aku akan memberikan kepadamu seekor unta dewasa, namun tidak diragukan lagi, itu tetap anak dari unta lain! Suatu ketika, beliau bercanda kepada Anas, "Wahai orang yang memiliki dua telinga!" Seorang wanita pernah datang kepada Nabi mengadukan suaminya, maka beliau berkata kepadanya, "Apakah suamimu yang matanya putih itu?" Wanita itu menjadi khawatir dan mengira suaminya telah buta, mengingat apa yang Allah firmankan tentang Nabi Ya'qub, "Matanya memutih karena banyak menangis..." maksudnya, beliau menjadi buta. Ia kembali kepada suaminya dengan ketakutan dan mulai memeriksa matanya dengan seksama. Suaminya bertanya apa masalahnya. Ia berkata, "Nabi mengatakan ada warna putih di matamu!" Lelaki itu berkata, "Wahai wanita! Apakah beliau tidak mengatakan kepadamu bahwa warna putih di mataku lebih banyak daripada warna hitam?" maksudnya; semua orang memiliki warna putih dan hitam di matanya. Nabi akan merespons dengan positif terhadap siapa pun yang bercanda atau bergurau dengannya dan beliau akan tersenyum.
content_ar: يستعرض المؤلف في هذا الجزء جوانب من فن التعامل مع الناس مستلهمًا ذلك من السيرة النبوية الشريفة. ويضرب أمثلة من أحاديث النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم وأفعاله في التعامل مع أصحابه والناس من حوله، بما يكشف عمق حكمته وسماحة أخلاقه صلى الله عليه وسلم.
